Student Welfare Counsellor Committee Anti Ragging Committee SC/ST Cell Contact STUDY IN INDIA X TNEACODE 2739 Campus tour Admission 2025 TNEACODE 2739 COUNSELLOR COMMITTEE Need Counselling / Guidance - Click Here Sri Eshwar is deeply committed to supporting the mental well-being of its community. A full-time counsellor, working under the guidance of the Head – Office of Student Welfare , is available to provide confidential support to students, faculty, and staff . The counsellor’s role focuses on: Providing professional guidance through one-on-one counselling sessions. Organizing awareness initiatives to promote mental health and emotional well-being. Creating a safe, supportive, and inclusive environment for individuals facing emotional, psychological, or personal challenges. STUDENT COUNSELLOR Ms.Bavithra Arumugam OBJECTIVES Personal Counseling (stress, anxiety, depression, homesickness, relationship and emotional issues, or any other personal issues) Therapeutic sessions (relaxation techniques, CBT, Art therapy, Play therapy, etc). Academic Counselling (facing academic challenges, feeling overwhelmed by coursework, or seeking guidance on career planning for future aspirations). Group Counselling (foster connections with peers who share similar experiences, promoting mutual support and growth). Conducting Workshops and Seminars for various topics such as stress management, time management, study skills, mental health awareness and personality development programs. Performing Psychometric Assessments to predict individual’s personalities and behavioural tendencies. Work-related stress (managing workload, time management, and work-life balance).
